data.intent_description: BGP adjacency is down because R3 (and peers R1/R2) have no BGP process configured; restore BGP adjacency and route exchange by configuring BGP on R3 and its peers.

plan.problem: No BGP process or neighbor configuration exists on R3 (and R1/R2), so BGP adjacencies cannot form and routes are not exchanged.
plan.fix_summary: Configure a BGP process on R3 and configure matching neighbor statements on R1 and R2 to establish BGP adjacencies. Assumptions made: use AS 65000 for all routers (iBGP), R3 reachable at 10.0.0.4, R1 at 10.0.0.1, R2 at 10.0.0.2, and R3 loopback 203.0.113.1 advertised via BGP. Operational risk: deploying BGP may change route propagation; validate planned AS/neighbor IPs before applying in production.

messages.content:
{
  "intent": "check_and_fix",
  "intent_description": "BGP adjacency is down because R3 (and peers R1/R2) have no BGP process configured; restore BGP adjacency and route exchange by configuring BGP on R3 and its peers.",
  "target": "R3",
  "needs_fix": true,
  "plan": {
    "problem": "No BGP process or neighbor configuration exists on R3 (and R1/R2), so BGP adjacencies cannot form and routes are not exchanged.",
    "fix_summary": "Configure a BGP process on R3 and configure matching neighbor statements on R1 and R2 to establish BGP adjacencies. Assumptions made: use AS 65000 for all routers (iBGP), R3 reachable at 10.0.0.4, R1 at 10.0.0.1, R2 at 10.0.0.2, and R3 loopback 203.0.113.1 advertised via BGP. Operational risk: deploying BGP may change route propagation; validate planned AS/neighbor IPs before applying in production.",
    "plan_steps": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "device": "router3",
        "action": "configure_bgp",
        "target": "AS 65000",
        "parameters": [
          "router bgp 65000",
          "bgp router-id 203.0.113.1",
          "network 203.0.113.1/32",
          "neighbor 10.0.0.1 remote-as 65000",
          "neighbor 10.0.0.2 remote-as 65000"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "device": "router1",
        "action": "configure_bgp",
        "target": "AS 65000",

messages.content:
{
  "intent": "check_and_fix",
  "intent_description": "BGP adjacencies are down because R3, R1, and R2 have no BGP process configured; restore iBGP/eBGP adjacencies so routes can be exchanged.",
  "target": "R3",
  "needs_fix": true,
  "plan": {
    "problem": "R3, R1, and R2 have no BGP process configured; peers cannot form BGP adjacencies and no routes are exchanged.",
    "fix_summary": "Create BGP process on router3, router1, and router2 with assumed AS numbers (R1:65001, R2:65002, R3:65003) and configure neighbor relationships using existing serial link IPs.",
    "plan_steps": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "device": "router3",
        "action": "create_bgp_process",
        "target": "65003",
        "parameters": [
          "asn:65003",
          "router_id:3.3.3.3",
          "neighbor 10.0.0.5 remote-as 65002",
          "neighbor 10.0.0.10 remote-as 65001",
          "address-family: ipv4-unicast"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "device": "router1",
        "action": "create_bgp_process",
        "target": "65001",
        "parameters": [
          "asn:65001",
          "router_id:1.1.1.1",
          "neighbor 10.0.0.9 remote-as 65003",
          "address-family: ipv4-unicast"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "device": "router2",
        "action": "create_bgp_process",
        "target": "65002",
        "parameters": [
          "asn:65002",
          "router_id:2.2.2.2",
          "neighbor 10.0.0.6 remote-as 65003",
          "address-family: ipv4-unicast"
        ]
      }
    ]

messages.content:
What was observed
- BGP adjacencies are down. Running-configs for R1, R2 and R3 contain OSPF and interfaces but no "router bgp" / BGP stanza. OSPF adjacencies and serial interfaces are up, so IP connectivity is OK.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Missing BGP configuration on R3, R1 and R2 — no BGP process to form adjacencies or exchange routes.

Changes made
- Configured interfaces (successful):
  - R3 Serial0/1/0 set to 10.0.0.9/30
  - R3 Serial0/1/1 set to 10.0.0.6/30
  - R2 Serial0/1/1 set to 10.0.0.5/30
- Failed attempts:
  - R3 loopback0 configuration failed (RESTCONF 400).
  - Attempts to create OSPF/BGP processes via RESTCONF failed (404) — no BGP process created on any router.

Verification
- Result: FAILED.
- Evidence: Post-change running-configs still show no BGP/"router bgp" stanza on R1, R2 or R3. Interfaces and OSPF adjacencies are up (IP-level connectivity confirmed).
- Remaining work: Create BGP processes on R1 (AS 65001), R2 (AS 65002) and R3 (AS 65003) and configure neighbor statements (R3 neighbors 10.0.0.5→65002 and 10.0.0.10→65001 per plan). Also re-try loopback/router-id configuration if required. After that, verify BGP adjacency state and route exchange.

If you want, I can push the exact "router bgp" configurations to the three routers now.
